findings.description: The title slide layout is not preserved. The PDF has a centered layout with the title, author info, and date positioned between two panoramic images. The HTML renders these elements left-aligned in a single-column flow with images and text separated.
findings.evidence: PDF: Title is centered in italic blue between two landscape photos with NC logo to the left. HTML: Content is left-aligned, images are smaller and stacked, title text appears below images rather than between them.

findings.description: Images on the title page are significantly smaller in the HTML compared to the PDF, where they span the full width of the slide.
findings.evidence: PDF: Two full-width panoramic images spanning the entire slide. HTML: Same images present but rendered at reduced width.

findings.description: The title text on page 1 loses its italic styling in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF: '15A NCAC 02K .0224 - Additional Requirements for Dams that Impound Coal Combustion Residuals' is in italic blue. HTML: Same text appears in bold non-italic dark font.

findings.description: Page 2 layout changes from a side-by-side arrangement (bullet list on left, image on right) to a stacked arrangement in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: Bullet points on left side, construction/coal image on right side. HTML: Bullets listed first, image below.

findings.description: The title 'DEMLR Dam Safety Rulemaking' loses its italic blue styling in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: Title in italic blue. HTML: Title in bold dark font.

findings.description: Page 3 title 'Dam Safety CCR Rulemaking Purpose' loses its italic blue styling.
findings.evidence: PDF: Italic blue title. HTML: Bold dark font title.

findings.description: Page 4 title 'Dam Safety CCR Rulemaking Methodology' loses its italic blue styling.
findings.evidence: PDF: Italic blue title. HTML: Bold dark font title.

findings.description: On page 5, the bold/underline emphasis on 'equal to or more restrictive' and 'more restrictive' is partially preserved but the underline formatting appears different.
findings.evidence: PDF: Bold underlined text. HTML: Bold text present but underline styling may differ.

findings.description: Page 7 regulatory impact analysis is rendered as a table in HTML but presented as aligned text pairs in the PDF. The data is preserved correctly.
findings.evidence: PDF: Label-value pairs in aligned columns. HTML: Formal table with headers, rows, and borders.

findings.description: Page 8 contact information is rendered as a formatted table in HTML but was aligned text in the PDF. Data is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF: Names, emails, and phone numbers in aligned columns. HTML: Formal table with Name/Email/Phone headers.

findings.description: The Regulatory Impact Analysis on page 7 of the PDF is presented as a simple label-value list, not a table. The HTML converts this into a formal table with headers 'Category' and 'Impact' that do not exist in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF page 7 shows a simple two-column layout without table borders or headers. HTML adds <thead> with <th>Category</th> and <th>Impact</th> which are fabricated column headers.

findings.description: The table header labels 'Category' and 'Impact' on the Regulatory Impact Analysis table are fabricated - they do not appear in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF page 7 lists items like 'State Government: Minimal Costs' without any column headers. HTML adds 'Category' and 'Impact' as <th> elements.

findings.description: The hyperlinks in the HTML on page 8 are rendered as plain text rather than clickable links. The PDF contained clickable hyperlinks for the URLs and email addresses.
findings.evidence: PDF page 8 has clickable links for 'https://deq.nc.gov/documents/15a-ncac-02k-0224...' and email addresses. The HTML renders these as plain text without <a> tags (though the contact table does not use anchor tags either).

findings.description: Page 6 uses both h2 and h3, where the h3 '15A NCAC 02K .0224 - Additional Requirements for Dams that Impound Coal Combustion Residuals' is actually the section subtitle/rule title that appears as bold text in the PDF, not a sub-heading under the main heading. This is a minor structural interpretation issue.
findings.evidence: PDF page 6 shows '15A NCAC 02K .0224 - Additional Requirements for Dams that Impound Coal Combustion Residuals' as bold body text, not a sub-heading.

findings.description: The HTML has two nested <main> elements, which is a structural duplication of the main container, though content itself is not duplicated.
findings.evidence: HTML source shows <main> containing another <main> element.
